text: # 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0003
text: Technology, Media and Telecom (TMT) is the sector where the new economy first becomes visible. IT services, the main industry within the sector, continues to grow strongly and has emerged as a driving force in the Dutch economy.

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0003
metadata.source_slug: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland
metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

text: - [State of Technology, Media and Telecom (TMT) (13 January 2020)

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0005

metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0006

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0006
metadata.source_slug: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland
metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0007
text: To keep contributing to the Netherlands’ earnings model, the preconditions must be in order. The first major challenge is 5G, for which the first Dutch auctions will take place this year. Unfortunately, the Netherlands is lagging behind many other countries. It is vital that the Dutch IT cluster and other segments of the economy make full use of the benefits of this new development.

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0007
metadata.source_slug: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland
metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0008
text: IT services – the largest industry in the Technology, Media and Telecom (TMT) sector – is an important driver of growth in the Dutch economy. In the first three quarters of 2019, revenue in this segment even grew by 7 per cent year-on-year. Software companies and hosting companies in particular – driven by rising demand for cloud-based services – are a major growth engine of IT services. Although ABN AMRO expects GDP in the Netherlands to grow by less than one per cent in 2020, revenue in the TMT sector is expected to rise by 3 per cent and the IT industry will perform even better (+4.5 per cent). The strong growth of this industry has led to a sharp increase in the number of employees in recent years. At the same time, this is also its biggest problem, as there is still a large shortage of IT staff.

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0008
metadata.source_slug: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland
metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0009
text: In telecoms, 2019 actually saw contraction (-0.5 per cent), partly due to digitalisation, the focus on data traffic and falling prices for telephony services. In 2020, ABN AMRO expects a further decline in telecoms revenue: -2.0 per cent. Other industries within the sector, such as publishers and printers, will also see a slight decline in revenue in the coming years.

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0009
metadata.source_slug: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland
metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0010
text: ## Introducing 5G is required to maintain the strong position of the Dutch IT sector

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0010
metadata.source_slug: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland
metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0011
text: The Dutch IT industry holds a very strong position worldwide thanks to robust infrastructure with fast internet connections. However, the Netherlands is lagging behind other countries, especially in Asia, when it comes to adopting 5G. This network offers much higher speeds than the current 4G network. For many applications, 4G still seems adequate at present and the Netherlands also has one of the fastest 4G networks in the world. That is why a rapid introduction of 5G does not seem necessary right now. However, the Netherlands cannot afford to be at the back when 5G developments truly gain momentum. “The first auctions for 5G will not take place in our country until 2020. It is vital that the Dutch IT sector catches up and makes full use of the benefits of 5G,” emphasises Kasper Buiting, Sector Economist Technology, Media and Telecom at ABN AMRO. “The much higher speeds of 5G, for example, bring promising revenue models and new applications, such as the self-driving car and VR services, within reach. Too slow a development of 5G could eventually harm competitiveness and the favourable business climate for IT companies in the Netherlands.”

metadata.chunk_id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0011
metadata.source_slug: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland
metadata.source_title: 5G essential for the Netherlands’ earning capacity

id: 5G essentieel voor het verdienvermogen van Nederland__a2-late-chunking__0015
text: Technology, Media & Telecom (TMT) is one of the fastest-growing sectors. Helped by the increasing use of software, algorithms and sensors, this sector is at the forefront of the digital renewal of other sectors.
